groups.title: Essentials
groups.description: Campaign subject, goal, audience, key message, tap destination, notification type, language, urgency, and variant count.

groups.title: Message & Delivery
groups.description: Timing context, expiration, title/body structure, tone, and emoji preference.

groups.title: Brand & Personalization
groups.description: Sender identity, brand voice, supported personalization tokens, and CTA wording preferences.

groups.title: Compliance & Restrictions
groups.description: Sensitive categories, jurisdiction, required disclosures, claim restrictions, and final requirements.

variables.label: What is the notification about?
variables.placeholder: Order update, new feature, expiring offer, appointment reminder
variables.hint: Name the event, update, product, offer, or action behind the notification.

help.what: Provide the what is the notification about? before generating push notification copy.
help.why: It establishes the central subject the notification must communicate.
help.example: Good example: Order update for a confirmed purchase
help.avoid: Avoid vague placeholders, fake urgency, fake personalization, clickbait, or changing the notification into a different subject.

variables.label: Primary goal
variables.placeholder: Select the main goal
variables.hint: Choose the single most important outcome for this notification.

help.what: Provide the primary goal before generating push notification copy.
help.why: The goal determines the message angle, urgency, and strength of the call to action.
help.avoid: Avoid vague placeholders, fake urgency, fake personalization, clickbait, or changing the notification into a different subject.

variables.label: Target audience
variables.placeholder: Existing customers who added an item to their cart but did not complete checkout
variables.hint: Describe who will receive the notification and any relevant context.

help.what: Provide the target audience before generating push notification copy.
help.why: Audience context affects relevance, wording, assumed knowledge, and privacy risk.
help.example: Good example: Existing customers waiting for an order update inside the mobile app.
help.avoid: Avoid vague placeholders, fake urgency, fake personalization, clickbait, or changing the notification into a different subject.

variables.label: Key message and facts
variables.placeholder: State what happened, what matters, and any verified dates, prices, or conditions
variables.hint: Include only facts that may appear in the notification.

help.what: Provide the key message and facts before generating push notification copy.
help.why: It provides the verified information the notification must communicate without fabrication.
help.example: Good example: The order has shipped and tracking is available from the order details screen.
help.avoid: Avoid invented prices, dates, features, availability, results, urgency, user behavior, or claims.

variables.label: Tap destination
variables.placeholder: Checkout screen, order details, article page, https://www.creatornivo.com/offers/summer
variables.hint: Enter the app screen, deep link, page, or action opened after the user taps.

help.what: Provide the tap destination before generating push notification copy.
help.why: A push notification should set an accurate expectation for what happens after the tap.
help.avoid: Avoid invented screens, broken links, misleading destinations, or tap expectations that the product cannot support.

variables.label: Notification type
variables.placeholder: Let Creatornivo decide
variables.hint: Select a type when it materially affects tone or compliance.

help.what: Optional context for the notification type field.
help.why: Different notification types require different levels of urgency, persuasion, and sender context.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, private user data, manipulative pressure, unsupported urgency, excessive emojis, or generic marketing filler.

variables.label: Output language
variables.placeholder: English, Spanish, German, Ukrainian
variables.hint: Specify the language or locale used in the final notification.

help.what: Optional context for the output language field.
help.why: Language affects brevity, tone, grammar, and visual character density.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, private user data, manipulative pressure, unsupported urgency, excessive emojis, or generic marketing filler.

variables.label: Urgency level
variables.placeholder: Select urgency
variables.hint: High or critical urgency will be used only when supported by the supplied facts.

help.what: Optional context for the urgency level field.
help.why: It controls pacing and emphasis while preventing unjustified urgency.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, private user data, manipulative pressure, unsupported urgency, excessive emojis, or generic marketing filler.

variables.label: Number of variants
variables.hint: Generate between one and five distinct notification options.

help.what: Optional context for the number of variants field.
help.why: It lets users choose between a single result and a small set of genuinely different angles.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, private user data, manipulative pressure, unsupported urgency, excessive emojis, or generic marketing filler.

variables.label: Timing context
variables.placeholder: Send 30 minutes before the appointment or after a failed payment
variables.hint: Describe when or after which event the notification should be sent.

help.what: Optional context for the timing context field.
help.why: Timing can change the relevance, tense, and urgency of the message.
help.example: Good example: Send after shipment is confirmed.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, private user data, manipulative pressure, unsupported urgency, excessive emojis, or generic marketing filler.

variables.label: Expiration or deadline
variables.placeholder: Valid until 6 PM on July 15, or remove after the event begins
variables.hint: Add a verified deadline or the point when the notification becomes irrelevant.

help.what: Optional context for the expiration or deadline field.
help.why: It prevents outdated notifications and supports accurate deadline wording.
help.example: Good example: Remove after the order is delivered.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, private user data, manipulative pressure, unsupported urgency, excessive emojis, or generic marketing filler.

variables.label: Notification structure
variables.placeholder: Let Creatornivo decide
variables.hint: Choose whether the output should use a title and body or body-only format.

help.what: Optional context for the notification structure field.
help.why: Some notification environments support a separate title, while others use one compact message.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, private user data, manipulative pressure, unsupported urgency, excessive emojis, or generic marketing filler.

variables.label: Tone
variables.placeholder: Select a tone
variables.hint: Choose the overall voice without changing the underlying facts.

help.what: Optional context for the tone field.
help.why: Tone materially affects how the same short message is perceived.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, private user data, manipulative pressure, unsupported urgency, excessive emojis, or generic marketing filler.

variables.label: Emoji use
variables.placeholder: Select emoji use
variables.hint: Emoji should support meaning rather than consume limited notification space.

help.what: Optional context for the emoji use field.
help.why: Emoji use is a recognizable brand and platform preference that cannot always be inferred.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, private user data, manipulative pressure, unsupported urgency, excessive emojis, or generic marketing filler.

variables.label: Sender or brand name
variables.placeholder: Northstar, Acme App, River Dental
variables.hint: Add the sender only when users may not recognize the app or message context.

help.what: Optional context for the sender or brand name field.
help.why: Sender identification can improve clarity, especially for browser or multi-brand notifications.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, private user data, manipulative pressure, unsupported urgency, excessive emojis, or generic marketing filler.

variables.label: Brand voice
variables.placeholder: Calm, concise, helpful, never overly promotional or playful
variables.hint: Describe distinctive voice preferences not covered by the tone selector.

help.what: Optional context for the brand voice field.
help.why: It preserves meaningful brand distinctions without creating several overlapping style fields.
help.example: Good example: Helpful, concise, and calm.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, private user data, manipulative pressure, unsupported urgency, excessive emojis, or generic marketing filler.

variables.label: Personalization tokens
variables.placeholder: {{first_name}}, {{order_number}}, {{appointment_time}}
variables.hint: List only tokens supported by your notification system.

help.what: Optional context for the personalization tokens field.
help.why: The model must preserve valid token syntax and avoid inventing unsupported personalization.
help.avoid: Avoid personal data or token names that the notification system cannot reliably insert.

variables.label: Call-to-action preference
variables.placeholder: View order, Continue checkout, Read update, or Auto
variables.hint: Suggest a preferred action phrase or leave Auto for context-based wording.

help.what: Optional context for the call-to-action preference field.
help.why: A specific action preference can materially change the final wording and tap expectation.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, private user data, manipulative pressure, unsupported urgency, excessive emojis, or generic marketing filler.

variables.label: Sensitive or regulated topic
variables.placeholder: Select only when relevant
variables.hint: This enables more cautious wording and relevant verification notes.

help.what: Optional context for the sensitive or regulated topic field.
help.why: Sensitive categories may require stricter privacy, claim, disclosure, and jurisdiction handling.
help.avoid: Avoid unsupported medical, financial, legal, employment, insurance, political, child-related, gambling, or regulated claims.

variables.label: Relevant jurisdiction
variables.placeholder: United States, California, European Union, United Kingdom
variables.hint: Enter the country or region whose rules may apply.

help.what: Optional context for the relevant jurisdiction field.
help.why: Regulatory wording can depend on the location in which the notification is used.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, private user data, manipulative pressure, unsupported urgency, excessive emojis, or generic marketing filler.

variables.label: Required disclosure
variables.placeholder: Provide approved legal, eligibility, risk, or promotional disclosure text
variables.hint: Use only supplied or approved wording; long disclosures may require another channel.

help.what: Optional context for the required disclosure field.
help.why: It allows mandatory wording to be considered without asking the model to invent legal language.
help.example: Good example: Use the approved eligibility disclosure supplied here.
help.avoid: Avoid unsupported medical, financial, legal, employment, insurance, political, child-related, gambling, or regulated claims.

variables.label: Claims and restrictions
variables.placeholder: Avoid guaranteed results; do not mention price; use the exact approved offer terms
variables.hint: List prohibited claims, required facts, or wording that must not appear.

help.what: Optional context for the claims and restrictions field.
help.why: It prevents unsupported, legally risky, inaccurate, or brand-inconsistent statements.
help.example: Good example: Do not mention price, discounts, or guaranteed delivery time.
help.avoid: Avoid invented prices, dates, features, availability, results, urgency, user behavior, or claims.

variables.label: Additional requirements
variables.placeholder: Add any important instruction not covered by the fields above
variables.hint: Use this for final requirements only, not general writing best practices.

help.what: Optional context for the additional requirements field.
help.why: It provides one controlled place for legitimate requirements that do not fit another field.
help.example: Good example: Avoid fake urgency and keep each variant understandable when truncated.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, private user data, manipulative pressure, unsupported urgency, excessive emojis, or generic marketing filler.
